summary.intent: Fix two bugs in the NumberInput component found in a Codex code review: a P1 controlled-mode issue where SetProps was self-authoring values, and a P2 issue where EndScrub failed when the input was disabled/readonly.
summary.outcome: Both bugs were fixed in number_input/mod.rs, 60 NumberInput tests pass, clippy clean, and changes were committed and pushed as round 8.

repo: Controlled-mode contract: SetProps must not self-author/clamp values — the caller owns the value
EndScrub must execute its cleanup even when the component is disabled or readonly
The project uses xci-fast as a fast CI check command before pushing

code.finding: Controlled-mode SetProps was incorrectly clamping/mutating values — fix ensures it passes values through without self-authoring
EndScrub had a disabled/readonly guard that was blocking scrub-end cleanup — guard was removed or moved so cleanup always runs

workflow: Background polling for PR reviews was unreliable — foreground polling caught new Codex review comments ~4 minutes after they were posted
Existing tests from a prior round (round 5) asserted controlled-mode clamping behavior that was invalidated by the P1 fix — contract changes require auditing prior test assertions

friction: Background polling was silently dropping new review notifications; reviews were 4 minutes old before they were caught, requiring a switch to foreground polling
open_items: Round-5 tests that asserted controlled-mode clamping behavior may need to be updated to match the new contract where SetProps does not self-author values — the assistant noted this but the transcript does not confirm those tests were updated
